cases.query: how do I stop severe bleeding

result.passage: For severe bleeding, apply firm direct pressure with clean cloth, gauze, or the cleanest available material. Keep pressure steady. Do not repeatedly lift the dressing to check the wound because that can restart bleeding.
result.title: Severe Bleeding

results.title: Basic Wound Care

cases.query: what should I do for burns

result.passage: Cool a burn with clean, cool running water for about 20 minutes if possible. Remove jewelry or tight clothing near the burn before swelling starts, but do not remove material stuck to the skin.
result.title: Burns

cases.query: what are signs of dehydration

result.passage: Warning signs include confusion, fainting, very little urine, rapid heartbeat, sunken eyes, inability to keep fluids down, or worsening weakness. These signs require urgent medical help when available.
result.title: Dehydration

results.passage: Warning signs include confusion, fainting, very little urine, rapid heartbeat, sunken eyes, inability to keep fluids down, or worsening weakness. These signs require urgent medical help when available.
results.title: Dehydration

results.passage: Prevent dehydration by drinking small amounts regularly, resting during the hottest part of the day, and reducing unnecessary exertion. Dark urine, dizziness, dry mouth, confusion, and inability to sweat can be warning signs.
results.title: Dehydration Prevention

results.passage: Hypothermia can occur when the body loses heat faster than it can produce it. Warning signs include intense shivering, clumsiness, confusion, slurred speech, drowsiness, and weak pulse.
results.title: Hypothermia

cases.query: how do I manage insulin and medicine in a blackout

result.passage: Prioritize medicines that prevent immediate harm, such as insulin, seizure medicine, heart medicine, asthma inhalers, blood pressure medicine, anticoagulants, and psychiatric medicines. Do not intentionally skip critical medicine to stretch supply unless a qualified clinician tells you to.
result.title: Medication Management During Outage

results.passage: If you need direction during a blackout and do not have a compass, use more than one method when possible. Treat improvised direction finding as approximate, then confirm with landmarks, maps, roads, stars, sunrise, sunset, or repeated checks.
results.title: Finding North Without a Compass

results.passage: Poor sanitation can cause illness quickly during a blackout, especially when water is limited. Separate drinking water, handwashing water, dirty water, trash, human waste, and food preparation areas as much as possible.
results.title: Sanitation and Hygiene During Outage

cases.query: which medicines should not be skipped when power is out

result.title: Medication Management During Outage

cases.query: how do I save phone battery

result.passage: To extend phone battery life, reduce screen brightness, enable low-power mode, turn off Bluetooth, Wi-Fi, mobile data, location, and background app refresh when not needed. Use airplane mode when communication is not currently required.
result.title: Battery Saving

results.passage: To extend phone battery life, reduce screen brightness, enable low-power mode, turn off Bluetooth, Wi-Fi, mobile data, location, and background app refresh when not needed. Use airplane mode when communication is not currently required.

results.passage: Use a whistle, mirror, flashlight, bright fabric, smoke only when fire risk is controlled, or large ground symbols in open areas. Avoid using up phone battery for constant signaling unless rescuers are nearby.
results.title: Emergency Signaling

results.passage: Preserve night vision by using dim light and avoiding unnecessary bright flashes. Keep one light source in a known location so it can be found in darkness.
results.title: Emergency Lighting

result.passage: Battery-powered carbon monoxide alarms are valuable during outages. If an alarm sounds, leave the building first, then seek help. Do not re-enter until the source is found and the space is safe.

cases.query: is food in the fridge safe after a power outage

result.passage: During a power outage, keep refrigerator and freezer doors closed as much as possible. A closed refrigerator can usually keep food cold for several hours, and a full freezer stays cold longer than a half-empty freezer. Opening the door repeatedly lets cold air escape quickly.
result.title: Food Safety During Power Outage

results.title: Compass Navigation

cases.query: find north without a compass during a blackout

result.passage: Night method: in the northern hemisphere, find the Big Dipper if visible. The two outer stars of its bowl point toward Polaris, the North Star. Polaris sits roughly above true north. If clouds, buildings, smoke, or light pollution block the sky, do not rely on this method.
result.title: Finding North Without a Compass

results.passage: Night method: in the northern hemisphere, find the Big Dipper if visible. The two outer stars of its bowl point toward Polaris, the North Star. Polaris sits roughly above true north. If clouds, buildings, smoke, or light pollution block the sky, do not rely on this method.
results.title: Finding North Without a Compass

results.passage: A compass points toward magnetic north, not always true north. Keep it away from phones, speakers, magnets, vehicles, and metal tools while taking a bearing.
results.title: Compass Navigation

results.passage: Poor sanitation can cause illness quickly during a blackout, especially when water is limited. Separate drinking water, handwashing water, dirty water, trash, human waste, and food preparation areas as much as possible.
results.title: Sanitation and Hygiene During Outage

cases.query: I am lost and not sure whether to keep walking

result.passage: If you become lost, stop moving unless staying put is immediately unsafe. Panic walking wastes energy and can move you farther from known routes.
result.title: If You Are Lost

results.passage: If you become lost, stop moving unless staying put is immediately unsafe. Panic walking wastes energy and can move you farther from known routes.
results.title: If You Are Lost

results.passage: Seek emergency medical care as soon as possible. Watch for shock: pale or clammy skin, weakness, rapid breathing, confusion, or loss of consciousness. Keep the person still and warm.
results.title: Severe Bleeding

results.passage: If you have ice, a cooler, or frozen water bottles, group high-risk foods together and keep them cold. Melted ice water can contaminate food if packaging leaks, so keep food sealed and drain dirty water away from eating surfaces.
results.title: Food Safety During Power Outage

cases.query: how can I signal rescuers

result.passage: Signals for rescuers should be simple, repeated, and easy to notice. Three repeated sounds, flashes, or marks are widely recognized as a distress pattern in many contexts.
result.title: Emergency Signaling

results.passage: Signals for rescuers should be simple, repeated, and easy to notice. Three repeated sounds, flashes, or marks are widely recognized as a distress pattern in many contexts.
results.title: Emergency Signaling

results.passage: Avoid making travel decisions from one weak clue. Metal objects, electrical equipment, moving water, wind, slopes, and poor visibility can mislead improvised methods. If direction is uncertain and staying put is safe, conserve energy, mark your location, and signal for help.
results.title: Finding North Without a Compass

cases.query: three flashes whistle or marks to get rescue attention

result.passage: Signals for rescuers should be simple, repeated, and easy to notice. Three repeated sounds, flashes, or marks are widely recognized as a distress pattern in many contexts.
result.title: Emergency Signaling

results.passage: Signals for rescuers should be simple, repeated, and easy to notice. Three repeated sounds, flashes, or marks are widely recognized as a distress pattern in many contexts.
results.title: Emergency Signaling

results.passage: Use visible and audible signals when safe: bright cloth, reflective surfaces, whistle blasts, lights, or ground markings. Conserve battery and energy.
results.title: If You Are Lost

results.passage: If water may be contaminated, bring it to a rolling boil for at least one full minute. At elevations above 2,000 meters, boil for at least three minutes. Let the water cool in a covered, clean container.
results.title: Water Purification

cases.query: how can I use an emergency radio during an outage

result.passage: Keep radio volume low and use headphones when possible. Store spare batteries with the radio, protected from moisture and extreme heat.
result.title: Emergency Radio

results.passage: Keep radio volume low and use headphones when possible. Store spare batteries with the radio, protected from moisture and extreme heat.
results.title: Emergency Radio

results.passage: If evacuation is possible, carry medicines with you rather than leaving them behind. If supply is running low, contact pharmacies, clinics, emergency services, shelters, or neighbors early, before the last dose is gone.
results.title: Medication Management During Outage

results.passage: During a power outage, keep refrigerator and freezer doors closed as much as possible. A closed refrigerator can usually keep food cold for several hours, and a full freezer stays cold longer than a half-empty freezer. Opening the door repeatedly lets cold air escape quickly.
results.title: Food Safety During Power Outage

cases.query: como purificar agua en un apagon

result.passage: Para purificar agua que puede estar contaminada, hiervala antes de beberla cuando tenga combustible seguro. Lleve el agua a hervor fuerte por al menos un minuto. En zonas de gran altura, hierva por al menos tres minutos. Deje enfriar el agua en un recipiente limpio y cubierto.
result.title: Agua segura durante un apagon

results.passage: Para purificar agua que puede estar contaminada, hiervala antes de beberla cuando tenga combustible seguro. Lleve el agua a hervor fuerte por al menos un minuto. En zonas de gran altura, hierva por al menos tres minutos. Deje enfriar el agua en un recipiente limpio y cubierto.
results.title: Agua segura durante un apagon

results.passage: If boiling is not possible, use a clean cloth to remove visible sediment, then use a known disinfectant method only when instructions and supplies are available. Mark treated water containers so untreated water is not confused with treated water.
results.title: Water Purification

results.passage: Store drinking water in clean, food-grade containers with tight lids. Keep containers closed, shaded, and away from fuel, chemicals, trash, and floodwater.
results.title: Water Storage

cases.query: agua sucia despues de un apagon y no se si hervirla

result.passage: Guarde el agua tratada separada del agua sin tratar. Marque los recipientes con claridad. No meta vasos, manos o utensilios sucios dentro del recipiente de agua potable; mejor vierta el agua.
result.title: Agua segura durante un apagon

results.passage: Guarde el agua tratada separada del agua sin tratar. Marque los recipientes con claridad. No meta vasos, manos o utensilios sucios dentro del recipiente de agua potable; mejor vierta el agua.
results.title: Agua segura durante un apagon

results.passage: No comparta rumores. Comparta solamente informacion observada directamente o confirmada por una fuente confiable.
results.title: Comunicacion de emergencia

results.passage: If boiling is not possible, use a clean cloth to remove visible sediment, then use a known disinfectant method only when instructions and supplies are available. Mark treated water containers so untreated water is not confused with treated water.
results.title: Water Purification

cases.query: necesito pedir ayuda en espanol durante una emergencia

result.passage: En una emergencia, use mensajes cortos y claros. Diga donde esta, cuantas personas necesitan ayuda, que peligro existe y que recursos tiene.
result.title: Comunicacion de emergencia

results.passage: En una emergencia, use mensajes cortos y claros. Diga donde esta, cuantas personas necesitan ayuda, que peligro existe y que recursos tiene.
results.title: Comunicacion de emergencia

results.passage: Hervir ayuda contra muchos organismos que causan enfermedad, pero no elimina combustible, metales pesados, sal, pesticidas ni otros quimicos. Si el agua huele a gasolina, solventes, aguas negras o quimicos, evitela si existe otra fuente mas segura.
results.title: Agua segura durante un apagon

results.passage: Battery, hand-crank, or solar radios can provide official emergency information when phones and internet fail. Use scheduled listening windows to conserve power.
results.title: Emergency Radio
